---
title: "Topics"
subtitle: "Distribution of topics across 1665–1886, focusing on 'Experiment'"
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import plotly.graph_objects as go

from itables import show as itables_show
import itables.options as itables_opt

itables_opt.classes = "display nowrap compact"
itables_opt.maxBytes = 0

DERIV = Path("data/derivatives")
topic_totals = pd.read_csv(DERIV / "topic_totals.csv")
topic_year = pd.read_csv(DERIV / "topic_year_counts.csv")
articles_per_year = pd.read_csv(DERIV / "articles_per_year.csv")
experiment_articles = pd.DataFrame(json.loads((DERIV / "experiment_articles.json").read_text()))

FOCUS = "Experiment"
ACCENT = "#d62728"
MUTED = "#a8b0bd"
```

## How often does each topic appear?

```{python}
#| label: fig-topic-totals
totals = topic_totals.sort_values("count")
colors = [ACCENT if t == FOCUS else MUTED for t in totals["topic"]]

fig = go.Figure(
    go.Bar(
        x=totals["count"],
        y=totals["topic"],
        orientation="h",
        marker_color=colors,
        hovertemplate="<b>%{y}</b><br>%{x:,} articles<extra></extra>",
    )
)
fig.update_layout(
    height=600,
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=10, b=40),
    xaxis_title="Articles",
    yaxis_title=None,
    plot_bgcolor="white",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig
```

The Royal Society used a handful of broad labels — *Article* and *Letter* dominate, while *Experiment* sits in the top five with **`{python} int(topic_totals.loc[topic_totals.topic==FOCUS, 'count'].iloc[0])`** entries.

## Topics over time

Lines stack to the total per year. Click a topic in the legend to isolate it.

```{python}
#| label: fig-topics-over-time
top_n = 8
top_topics = topic_totals.nlargest(top_n, "count")["topic"].tolist()
plot_df = topic_year.copy()
plot_df["topic_grouped"] = plot_df["topic"].where(plot_df["topic"].isin(top_topics), "Other")

stacked = (
    plot_df.groupby(["year", "topic_grouped"], as_index=False)["count"].sum()
)
ordered = top_topics + ["Other"]
stacked["topic_grouped"] = pd.Categorical(stacked["topic_grouped"], categories=ordered, ordered=True)
stacked = stacked.sort_values(["year", "topic_grouped"])

colors_map = {t: c for t, c in zip(ordered, px.colors.qualitative.Set2 + ["#cccccc"])}
colors_map[FOCUS] = ACCENT

fig = px.area(
    stacked,
    x="year",
    y="count",
    color="topic_grouped",
    category_orders={"topic_grouped": ordered},
    color_discrete_map=colors_map,
)
fig.update_layout(
    height=480,
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=10, b=40),
    legend_title_text="Topic",
    xaxis_title="Year",
    yaxis_title="Articles per year",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    hovermode="x unified",
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig
```

## Experiment over time {.tabset}

```{python}
#| label: experiment-stats
exp_by_year = (
    topic_year.loc[topic_year["topic"] == FOCUS, ["year", "count"]]
    .sort_values("year")
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)
total_exp = int(exp_by_year["count"].sum())
first_year = int(exp_by_year["year"].min())
last_year = int(exp_by_year["year"].max())
peak_row = exp_by_year.loc[exp_by_year["count"].idxmax()]
peak_year = int(peak_row["year"])
peak_count = int(peak_row["count"])
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**`{python} total_exp`** articles tagged *Experiment* between **`{python} first_year`** and **`{python} last_year`**. Peak year: **`{python} peak_year`** (`{python} peak_count` articles).
:::

### Articles per year

```{python}
#| label: fig-experiment-count
exp_by_year["rolling5"] = exp_by_year["count"].rolling(window=5, center=True, min_periods=1).mean()

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(
    go.Bar(
        x=exp_by_year["year"],
        y=exp_by_year["count"],
        name="Experiment articles",
        marker_color=ACCENT,
        opacity=0.55,
        hovertemplate="%{x}: %{y} articles<extra></extra>",
    )
)
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=exp_by_year["year"],
        y=exp_by_year["rolling5"],
        mode="lines",
        name="5-year rolling mean",
        line=dict(color=ACCENT, width=2.5),
    )
)
fig.update_layout(
    height=420,
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=10, b=40),
    xaxis_title="Year",
    yaxis_title="Articles",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
)
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig.update_yaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig
```

### Share of all articles

Yearly publication volume changes a lot across two centuries; this view normalizes Experiment counts against the total in each year.

```{python}
#| label: fig-experiment-share
share = articles_per_year.merge(exp_by_year[["year", "count"]].rename(columns={"count": "experiment"}), on="year", how="left")
share["experiment"] = share["experiment"].fillna(0)
share["share"] = share["experiment"] / share["count"]
share["share_rolling5"] = share["share"].rolling(window=5, center=True, min_periods=1).mean()

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=share["year"],
        y=share["share"],
        mode="lines",
        name="Share",
        line=dict(color=ACCENT, width=1, dash="dot"),
        opacity=0.55,
        hovertemplate="%{x}: %{y:.1%}<extra></extra>",
    )
)
fig.add_trace(
    go.Scatter(
        x=share["year"],
        y=share["share_rolling5"],
        mode="lines",
        name="5-year rolling mean",
        line=dict(color=ACCENT, width=2.5),
    )
)
fig.update_layout(
    height=420,
    margin=dict(l=20, r=20, t=10, b=40),
    xaxis_title="Year",
    yaxis_title="Experiment share of articles",
    plot_bgcolor="white",
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=".0%", showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig.update_xaxes(showgrid=True, gridcolor="#eee")
fig
```

## Every Experiment article

Sort or search this table. Click a title to open the article on royalsocietypublishing.org.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-experiment
def linkify(row):
    label = row["title"] or "(untitled)"
    url = row["canonical_url"] if pd.notna(row["canonical_url"]) and row["canonical_url"] else None
    if not url and pd.notna(row["doi"]) and row["doi"]:
        url = f"https://doi.org/{row['doi']}"
    if url:
        return f'<a href="{url}" target="_blank" rel="noopener">{label}</a>'
    return label

def fmt_authors(authors):
    if not authors:
        return ""
    return "; ".join(authors)

table = experiment_articles.copy()
table["title_link"] = table.apply(linkify, axis=1)
table["authors_str"] = table["authors"].apply(fmt_authors)
display_df = table[["year", "title_link", "authors_str", "doi"]].rename(
    columns={"year": "Year", "title_link": "Title", "authors_str": "Authors", "doi": "DOI"}
)
itables_show(display_df, lengthMenu=[10, 25, 50, 100], paging=True)
```
